reasoning: Based on the expected behavior of equity prices over a roughly three-month period (from late October 2025 to January 2026). Given a normal positive drift in the market, equities generally have about a 53-55% chance of being higher over a 3-month horizon. As a REIT, FRT pays a meaningful quarterly dividend which mildly reduces the price-return drift relative to non-dividend-paying stocks. Factoring in expected annual price drift of ~3% (net of dividends) and annual volatility of ~15-20%, the z-score yields a probability just above 50%, roughly 53%.
